D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Adams Zone System — I to X

Ansel Adams divided the tonal scale into 11 zones, each one stop apart; zone V is mid-grey (18% reflectance), the anchor for every metering decision

The Zone System places every brightness in a scene on an 11-step ladder. Each zone is one stop (factor of 2) brighter than the previous. Zone V is the 18% mid-grey that a reflective meter assumes — “place” a subject on a zone by metering it, then exposing relative to mid-grey. Zone 0 is paper black, Zone X is paper white with no texture.

The strip below shows all 11 zones with their canonical descriptions. Drag the exposure slider — the scene moves up and down the zone scale, and the shadow/highlight clip indicators flag whether texture is preserved or lost.
